post.summary:
POST create_device — register a device, refresh its token, or file a plan
request. The app reuses this one endpoint for all three

requested_plan.description:
Purchase intent (Fawateer): kept permissive on purpose — a 422 here
would fail registration outright in the shipped app.

is_verified.description:
isActive(), not the raw column: legacy only forced is_verified to 0
past expiry on check_device, so create_device could answer
"verified" alongside an expires_at in the past. Harmless while every
device was operator-activated; with trials it means a device whose
trial has lapsed re-registers and is told it is verified. Both
endpoints now answer with one definition.

post.summary: POST check_device — current subscription status (is_verified forced 0 past expiry)

post.description:
Every field beyond the device key is optional: the app rotates its push
token by sending fcm_token alone, and edits the profile by sending
full_name/phone alone. Requiring all of them 422'd token rotation, which
silently cost the device its live-unlock push.

post.summary: POST add_review — store a rating/comment on the device

post.summary: POST add_review — store a rating/comment on the device

get.summary: GET getDevice — list all devices (legacy shape)

get.description:
Backs the operator console: `status=pending` is the work queue (purchase
intents filed from the app), `app_name` separates the products sharing this
deployment, and `q` searches the columns an operator has to hand when a
customer contacts them over WhatsApp — their phone, name, or device id.

get.description:
The same config catalog the apps see, so the operator can only pick a plan
id the device will actually recognise. Staff-scoped rather than reusing the
legacy shim's public getPlans, which Phase E retires.
get.summary:
GET /api/v1/device-subscriptions/plans — the catalog the operator activates
against

ActivateProductLicenseRequest.description:
A product self-activating one of its license's device/domain slots. The product
itself is identified by its API key (the `product` guard), not this body.
ActivateProductLicenseRequest.title: ActivateProductLicenseRequest

DeactivateProductLicenseRequest.description: A product releasing one of its license's activation slots by identifier.
DeactivateProductLicenseRequest.title: DeactivateProductLicenseRequest

status.description: The open purchase intent the operator acts on (see the console).

IdentifierType.description: How a subscription's product instance is identified — a web domain or a mobile/device id (the "domain or mobile id" from the dashboard requirements).
IdentifierType.title: IdentifierType

IssueOfflineTokenRequest.description:
A product requesting a signed offline token for one of its already-activated
devices (ADR 0005).
IssueOfflineTokenRequest.title: IssueOfflineTokenRequest

Platform.description: The target platform an artifact runs on. `Any` is a platform-agnostic artifact (e.g. a cross-platform archive or a firmware image).
Platform.title: Platform

key.description: Only ever populated once, at creation — never re-derivable afterwards.

method.description: `card` is collected asynchronously through Stripe (ADR 0009), not here.

ReleaseChannel.description: The release channel an artifact is published on. Products subscribe to a channel and self-update from its latest published release (ADR 0008).
ReleaseChannel.title: ReleaseChannel

ValidateProductLicenseRequest.description:
A product checking a license online. An optional `identifier` lets the platform
record the device/domain checking in (heartbeat) during validation.
ValidateProductLicenseRequest.title: ValidateProductLicenseRequest

errors.description: A detailed description of each field that failed validation.
